a.xlink:title: audit_events Append-only audit trail. Retained indefinitely.

bed_availability
Append-only table — rows are never UPDATEd or DELETEd by the application. Each row is a point-in-time snapshot of bed occupancy for one shelter + population type. beds_available is derived at query time: beds_total - beds_occupied - beds_on_hold (never stored).
Concurrent insert safety: the UNIQUE constraint on (shelter_id, population_type, snapshot_ts) combined with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING silently drops duplicate inserts when two coordinators submit at the exact same millisecond for the same shelter/population type.
Protected by PostgreSQL RLS (V13): inherits DV-shelter visibility through a join to the shelter table's RLS policy.

reservation
Soft-hold bed reservation. Lifecycle: HELD → CONFIRMED | CANCELLED | EXPIRED. Terminal states are immutable. Creating a hold increments beds_on_hold via a new availability snapshot. Confirming converts to beds_occupied. Cancelling or expiring decrements beds_on_hold. Hold duration is configurable per tenant (default 90 minutes) via tenant.config JSONB field hold_duration_minutes.
Auto-expiry: Lite tier uses @Scheduled polling (30s interval). Standard/Full tiers use Redis TTL with keyspace notification for near-instant expiry.
Protected by PostgreSQL RLS (V15): inherits DV-shelter visibility through a join to the shelter table's RLS policy.

surge_event
First-class domain entity for emergency surge events (White Flag nights, disaster response, seasonal overflow). Lifecycle: ACTIVE → DEACTIVATED (manual) or EXPIRED (scheduled_end passed). One active surge per tenant. surge.activated and surge.deactivated events published to EventBus.

referral_token
VAWA-compliant opaque referral. Contains zero client PII. Hard-deleted within 24 hours of terminal status. RLS inherits DV shelter access via shelter FK.

hmis_audit_log
Append-only audit log of HMIS data transmissions. Required by HMIS security standards.

bed_search_log
Every bed search is logged here — especially zero-result searches. Zero-result search count is the strongest unmet demand signal: "47 searches for SINGLE_ADULT beds with zero results last Tuesday night." Used for HUD grant applications and operational planning.

daily_utilization_summary
Pre-aggregated utilization summary populated by Spring Batch daily aggregation job (3 AM). Analytics dashboard queries hit this table instead of raw bed_availability snapshots. One row per (tenant, shelter, population_type, date).

notification
Persistent notification for in-app bell/inbox. Created by NotificationPersistenceService.send() on domain events (e.g. DV referral lifecycle). Protected by PostgreSQL RLS: recipients can only SELECT/UPDATE their own notifications. DELETE and INSERT are unrestricted (service-level).
